questions.question: What is the key difference between a Social Proof Library and a random 'testimonials' folder?

options.text: A library is stored in the cloud instead of locally
A library is organized by deployment context, not just by type or date
A library contains only video testimonials
A library requires a paid tool like Airtable

questions.explanation: A Social Proof Library is organized for deployment — tagged by type, industry, and metric — so you can find the right proof in under 30 seconds. Random folders are organized by how you collected proof, not how you'll use it.

questions.question: What are the three axes of the Social Proof Library organization system?

options.text: Proof type, customer context (industry/size), and strength/metric type

questions.explanation: The 3-axis system tags every piece of proof by: (1) Proof type (testimonial, case study, video, etc.), (2) Customer context (industry, company size, use case), and (3) Strength/metric type (revenue growth, time saved, churn reduced, etc.).

questions.question: Which type of social proof is most appropriate for the 'Decision' stage of the buyer journey?

options.text: Detailed case studies and video testimonials

questions.explanation: At the Decision stage, buyers need detailed proof: full Challenge-Solution-Results case studies and video testimonials that show real people achieving measurable outcomes. Logos and headline metrics are for Awareness, short quotes for Consideration.

questions.question: Every piece of customer-facing content should include at least one proof element — no exceptions.
questions.explanation: True. The Social Proof Insertion Rule states that every piece of customer-facing content must include at least one proof element. Landing pages with 3+ testimonials convert 34% better than those without.

questions.question: Describe how you would set up your Social Proof Library, including the tool you would use, the properties/tags you would track, and the views/filters you would create. What is your plan for populating it during the 90-minute migration sprint?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response selects a specific tool (Notion, Airtable, or Google Sheets), defines tags aligned with the 3-axis system (type, industry, metric), creates deployment-ready views, and outlines a concrete plan for the 90-minute migration sprint (gather, filter, tag, polish, deploy).
